id.description: An identifier established by the client.

jsonrpc.description: A string specifying the JSON-RPC protocol version.

method.description: A string containing the method name to be invoked.

params.description: A structure that holds the parameter values.

folder.description: Folder path that you would like to import report config to. Will be the root folder if the left blank, or the parent directory if the provided directory does not exist. e.g. provided folder path='/web/vancouver', '/web' exists, but '/web/vancouver' does not, the report configuration will be imported to '/web'.

jsonrpc.description: A string specifying the JSON-RPC protocol version.

method.description: A string containing the method name to be invoked.

params.description: A structure that holds the parameter values.

data-type.description: One option to download the text format of XML or CSV reports, e.g. 'text'.

format.description: One format got by /report/adom/<adom-name>/reports/state. Such as HTML, PDF, XML, CSV.

session.description: The cookie of an active session in FortiAnalyzer.

id.description: An identifier established by the client.

jsonrpc.description: A string specifying the JSON-RPC protocol version.

result.description: A structured value responding to report run method.

method.description: Checksum algorithm, e.g. 'MD5'.

data.description: Zipped and Base64 encoded data by default. Plain text for XML and CSV when specified in request.

data-type.description: The return type of data, e.g. 'text' or 'zip/base64'.

length.description: Length of the returned data stream.

name.description: Name of the report, e.g. 'Security Analysis'.

id.description: An identifier established by the client.

jsonrpc.description: A string specifying the JSON-RPC protocol version.

method.description: A string containing the method name to be invoked.

params.description: A structure that holds the parameter values.

sort-by.description: Sort by fields. Only support one field for this API.

field.description: Only support title currently. If there is no 'sort-by', then sort by start time of running report.

time-range.description: Time range for source data selection. It is required only if "state" is "generated".

end.description: Ending date time. Consider it following the timezone parameter's, ADOM's or FortiAnalyzer's timezone if timezone info is not specified within time range. Format: 'yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ' (RFC 3339) e.g. '2016-10-17T20:45:37-07:00 or 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' e.g. '2016-10-17 20:45:37'

start.description: Starting date time. Consider it following the timezone parameter's, ADOM's or FortiAnalyzer's timezone if timezone info is not specified within time range. Format: 'yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ' (RFC 3339) e.g. '2016-10-17T20:45:37-07:00 or 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' e.g. '2016-10-17 20:45:37'

timezone.description: The timezone index or name. Time range in request and date/time if any in response will follow this timezone. See Appendix

session.description: The cookie of an active session in FortiAnalyzer.

id.description: An identifier established by the client.

jsonrpc.description: A string specifying the JSON-RPC protocol version.

result.description: A structured value responding to report run method.

data.description: A structure that holds the parameter values.

device.description: A list of device names included in the report.

format.description: A list of report format, such as HTML, PDF, XML, CSV.

period-end.description: End time of original date included in this report, YYYY/MM/DD HH:mm

period-start.description: Start time of original date included in this report, YYYY/MM/DD HH:mm

tid.description: A unique ID of this report, you can use it to download or delete this report's data after this report's state is done.

id.description: An identifier established by the client.

jsonrpc.description: A string specifying the JSON-RPC protocol version.

method.description: A string containing the method name to be invoked.

params.description: A structure that holds the parameter values.

schedule.description: Schedule name or id. Required if schedule parameters are not included.

schedule-param.description: Schedule parameters. If the schedule name is given, the parameters will override the original report ones; If not, the layout-id, device, and time-period are required parameters to initiate a report.

period-end.description: End time of report data (hh:mm:ss YYYY/MM/DD).

period-start.description: Start time of report data (hh:mm:ss YYYY/MM/DD).

time-period.description: The type of time period. If 'other' is the type, then 'period-start' and 'period-end' must be specificed; If 'last-n-hours', 'last-n-days', or 'last-n-weeks' is selected, then 'period-last-n' field must be filled.

timezone.description: The timezone index or name. Time range in request and date/time if any in response will follow this timezone. See Appendix

info.description:
Documentation for FortiAnalyzer JSON API

This daemon controls main system functions, including log in and log out. All APIs for this module are under the prefix /sys/.
info.title: FortiAnalyzer JSON API Documentation

post.description: Request of importing report config files.
Request to add a graph file.
Request to get the info of a graph file.
Request to download a graph file.
Request to get the info of graph files.
Request to download a generated report.
It will list all reports in pending-running or generated state.
Start a task to run a report.
It will cancel a running report by task ID.
Get the task state of a report by task ID.
Request of deleting language sepcified report templates file.
Request of exporting report templates to file.
Request of installing language sepcified report templates from file.
